raw_consumer_statement:
I stayed at XXXX XXXX XXXX in late XX/XX/XXXX. My hotel XXXX states charges of {$2300.00}. The hotel XXXX shows XXXX payments made on my XXXX card- {$1700.00} and {$580.00}, total paid of {$2300.00}, bringing my account balance to zero ( see attached XXXX from hotel ). 

My card statement from XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX  Card XXXX in XXXX showed XXXX charges for this XXXX : {$1700.00} XXXX time, a second {$1700.00}, and {$580.00}, totaling {$4100.00} paid. I contacted Barclays and reported the duplicated payment of {$1700.00}. A credit was issued to my account by Barclays XXXX XX/XX/XXXX. 

XXXX 's, on XX/XX/XXXX, reversed the credit and the duplicate {$1700.00} charge reappeared on my card. Upon review of my XXXX statement, I noticed the charge had been reestablished and I called XXXX 's on XX/XX/XXXX to inquire why the credit reappeared. The agent claimed that XXXX 's had received support from the XXXX that supported the reason why the duplicate charge was reestablished. I made the agent aware that there must be an error and I asked for such documentation be sent to me. When I received the documentation, it did not support the Bank 's position at all. 

I called Barclays again, only to be advised that the case was closed and there is nothing that could be done. On XX/XX/XXXX I wrote a letter to XXXX 's again explaining the details and asking that they reopen the case to resolve the matter that I have been overcharged by {$1700.00}. 

I contact XXXX XXXX XXXX accounting department, spoke to the accounting XXXX XXXX XXXX, who stated that their records show only {$2300.00} was received from Barclays and they do not have record of ever receiving the additional {$1700.00}. See their reply email dated XX/XX/XXXX. 

In a letter dated XX/XX/XXXX, Barclays again advises that they " remain unable to further pursue '' this case and directs me to pursue issue with merchant ( which I have already ). 

I am stuck between a merchant who claims to have not received the duplicate payment and a XXXX  card company that states everything is proper and will not investigate further. XXXX 's has not given me a proper explanation why they feel it was appropriate to reestablish the XXXX charge. 

As a consumer, I am hoping the CFPB can help me find out who has my {$1700.00}. I have been charged and have paid {$1700.00} XXXX times -- XXXX legit and XXXX duplicate. 

What can I do from here? Please help!! 

XXXX XXXX XXXX
